title: KROMA module manifest
description: Schema for a module's module.json (and the YAML frontmatter of a .module.md).

id.description: Reverse-DNS identifier, e.g. tv.kroma.torrents. The runtime join key.

minServer.description: Minimum KROMA server version the module needs: a bare version ("0.2.0" = at least 0.2.0) or a full semver range (">=0.2, <0.4"). Checked at install and spawn; absent = any server.

library.description: A library module: its `.kmod` ships no native binary (its code is co-linked into the processes that need it), so the supervisor registers it but spawns no process. E.g. the release-name parser.

adminPrefixes.description: First path segments under /api/admin the module's sidecar owns (e.g. "downloads"); the core reverse-proxies /api/admin/<seg>/* to it.

dependsOn.description: Hard dependencies as a package.json-style map { "<id>": "<semver range>" } (a bare "*" means any version). A legacy array of ids / 'id@range' strings / { id, version } objects is also accepted. Enforced on the backend.

optionalDependsOn.description: Soft dependencies (same shape as dependsOn): version-checked and ordered first when present, but not required.

requires.description: Capability dependencies, satisfied by any module providing the (kind[, id]).

label.description: Display name / i18n key shown in the admin add-picker (engine capabilities).

flow.description: A non-form add-flow discriminator (e.g. "definition"); the host page renders it.

fields.description: The add-form schema the admin renders for this engine.

feRemote.description: Frontend Module Federation remote (runtime-loaded modules). The entry URL is derived by the server as /modules/<id>/remoteEntry.js.
